odata-gateway-031.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Converting an FS into an OData TS produces a precise entity, navigation, filter and authorization specification ready for SEGW.

Used for:
- Ensuring developers, testers and security all see the same contract
- Locking down EDM types and mandatory filters before coding
- Documenting which auth objects DPC_EXT must enforce

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- Entities: SALESORDER (VBAK, key VBELN), SALESORDERITEM (VBAP, VBELN + POSNR)
- Property mapping: KUNAG -> CustomerID, NETWR -> NetAmount, ERDAT -> OrderDate
- Filters: ERDAT mandatory, KUNAG optional
- Auths: S_SERVICE ZSALES_SRV_0001, V_VBAK_VKO on VKORG/VTWEG/SPART

Interview keyword: FS says what, TS pins down entities, filters and auths.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between Functional Spec and Technical Spec in SAP Fiori OData context?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Functional Spec describe karti hai ki system business terms mein kya karna chahiye — business language mein entity names, filter requirements, user stories, UI flow. Technical Spec ise implementation design mein translate karti hai — ABAP table mappings, properties aur data types ke saath OData entity types, DPC_EXT method logic, filter implementation, authorization object requirements, aur error handling. TS developer ka design document hai jo FS ko actual ABAP code se bridge karta hai aur development shuru hone se pehle functional consultant ke saath review hoti hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The Functional Spec describes what the system should do in business terms — entity names in business language, filter requirements, user stories, UI flow. The Technical Spec translates this to implementation design — ABAP table mappings, OData entity types with properties and data types, DPC_EXT method logic, filter implementation, authorization object requirements, and error handling. The TS is the developer's design document that bridges the FS to actual ABAP code and is reviewed with the functional consultant before development begins.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: In practice, FS quality varies. Some FSes are detailed and precise; some are vague. Developer's job is to fill gaps through FS review meetings — 'FS says show sales orders but doesn't specify which fields — I need to confirm field list with the functional consultant before writing TS.'
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Directly coding from FS without writing TS — this causes rework when assumptions are wrong. TS forces you to think through the design before coding and surfaces gaps early.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FS → TS mapping example:
" FS: 'Show purchase orders filtered by vendor and date'
" TS:
" Entity: PURCHASEORDER from EKKO
" Key: PurchaseOrder (EBELN, Edm.String)
" Properties: Vendor (LIFNR), OrderDate (BEDAT), Total (NETWR)
" Filter: BEDAT (mandatory date range) + LIFNR (optional)
" Operation: GET_ENTITYSET
" Auth: M_BEST_BSA (BSART), M_BEST_EKO (EKORG)

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you identify which ABAP tables to use when reading a Functional Spec?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Main FS se business objects ko business module ke basis pe SAP standard tables ke saath map karta hoon. Sales ke liye: orders ke liye VBAK/VBAP, deliveries ke liye LIKP/LIPS, billing ke liye VBRK/VBRP. Procurement ke liye: EKKO/EKPO. FI ke liye: BKPF/BSEG. Customer master ke liye: KNA1/KNB1. Agar FS aisa business object mention kare jiska main familiar nahi hoon, main SE11 mein relevant business terms se search karta hoon, functional consultant se consult karta hoon, ya standard programs (SE38) check karta hoon dekhne ke liye ki woh similar functionality ke liye kaunse tables use karte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: I map business objects from the FS to SAP standard tables based on the business module. For sales: VBAK/VBAP for orders, LIKP/LIPS for deliveries, VBRK/VBRP for billing. For procurement: EKKO/EKPO. For FI: BKPF/BSEG. For customer master: KNA1/KNB1. If the FS mentions a business object I am not familiar with, I search SE11 using relevant business terms, consult the functional consultant, or check standard programs (SE38) to see what tables they use for similar functionality.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Knowing standard table mappings is a core ABAP skill that directly speeds up TS writing. Most Fiori apps are on standard business processes — sales, procurement, finance — and the table mappings are fixed. Memorize the top 10.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Inventing table names or assuming wrong tables — SELECT from VBAK when FS says 'delivery' (should be LIKP). Always confirm with functional consultant for non-obvious mappings.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Standard table mapping reference:
" Sales Order:      VBAK (header), VBAP (items)
" Delivery:         LIKP (header), LIPS (items)
" Billing:          VBRK (header), VBRP (items)
" Purchase Order:   EKKO (header), EKPO (items)
" Customer:         KNA1 (general), KNB1 (company code)
" Material:         MARA (general), MAKT (description)
" FI Document:      BKPF (header), BSEG (items)
" Cost Center:      CSKS
" GL Account:       SKA1

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you translate a filter requirement from FS to DPC_EXT implementation?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS se: 'Order date range se filter karo' → TS: property ORDERDATE (ERDAT), supported operators: ge, le (date range). DPC_EXT implementation: get_filter_select_options use karke io_tech_request_context se filter padho, ORDERDATE ranges extract karo, validate karo ki at least start date provided hai (mandatory filter), extracted ranges ko SELECT WHERE clause mein use karo. Agar FS kahe ki filter optional hai, validation skip karo but provided hai toh still apply karo. Har FS filter map karta hai: property name → ABAP field → get_filter_select_options key → SELECT range.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: From FS: 'Filter by order date range' → TS: property ORDERDATE (ERDAT), supported operators: ge, le (date range). DPC_EXT implementation: read filter from io_tech_request_context using get_filter_select_options, extract ORDERDATE ranges, validate that at least a start date is provided (mandatory filter), use extracted ranges in SELECT WHERE clause. If FS says filter is optional, skip validation but still apply if provided. Each FS filter maps to: property name → ABAP field → get_filter_select_options key → SELECT range.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Translating each FS filter to code requires understanding both the business intent and the ABAP implementation. Missing a filter in implementation means users can't filter — FS review meetings should verify each filter is implemented before UAT.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Hard-coding filter values in SELECT instead of reading from io_tech_request_context — common in beginners. Always read filters dynamically from the request context.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FS filter → TS → DPC_EXT
" FS: 'Filter by order date range (mandatory)'
" TS: property ORDERDATE, Edm.DateTime, mandatory

" DPC_EXT:
METHOD salesorderset_get_entityset.
  DATA(lt_filter) = io_tech_request_context->get_filter_select_options( ).
  READ TABLE lt_filter WITH KEY property = 'ORDERDATE'
    ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<date_f>).
  IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR <date_f>-select_options IS INITIAL.
    RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE /iwbep/cx_mgw_busi_exception
      EXPORTING message = 'Order date filter required'.
  ENDIF.
  SELECT vbeln kunag netwr erdat FROM vbak
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_vbak)
    WHERE erdat IN @<date_f>-select_options
    UP TO 500 ROWS.
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle navigation properties in OData when the FS says 'show order with line items'?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS requirement 'order ko line items ke saath dikhao' OData TS mein navigation property mein translate hota hai. Main do entity types banata hoon — VBAK se SalesOrder aur VBAP se SalesOrderItem — phir VBELN pe 1:N association define karta hoon, AssociationSet banata hoon, aur SalesOrder entity mein NavigationProperty SalesOrderItems add karta hoon. DPC_EXT mein, GET_ENTITYSET orders return karta hai; jab $expand=SalesOrderItems requested ho, navigation property handler relevant orders ke liye VBAP data fetch karta hai. Yeh Fiori ko single HTTP call mein parent aur child load karne deta hai, round trips reduce karte hue.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: FS requirement 'show order with line items' translates to a navigation property in OData TS. I create two entity types — SalesOrder from VBAK and SalesOrderItem from VBAP — then define a 1:N association on VBELN, create an AssociationSet, and add a NavigationProperty SalesOrderItems to the SalesOrder entity. In DPC_EXT, GET_ENTITYSET returns orders; when $expand=SalesOrderItems is requested, the navigation property handler fetches VBAP data for the relevant orders. This allows Fiori to load parent and child in a single HTTP call, reducing round trips.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Navigation properties with $expand are one of the most powerful OData features. Performance tip: when implementing $expand, use a single JOIN query rather than fetching items per order in a loop — this is critical for large datasets.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Forgetting to handle $expand in DPC_EXT — the navigation exists in metadata but returns empty. SEGW defines the structure; DPC_EXT must actually implement the data fetching for navigation expansion.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SEGW: Association definition
" Association: SalesOrderToItems
" Principal Entity: SalesOrder (1)
" Dependent Entity: SalesOrderItem (N)
" Principal Key: SalesOrder (VBELN)
" Dependent Key: SalesOrder (VBELN)

" Fiori call with $expand:
" GET /SalesOrderSet('0000012345')?$expand=SalesOrderItems

" DPC_EXT handles navigation:
METHOD salesorderitem_get_entityset.
  " Called when $expand=SalesOrderItems requested
  DATA(lt_source_keys) = io_tech_request_context->get_source_keys( ).
  DATA(lv_order) = lt_source_keys[ name = 'SalesOrder' ]-value.
  SELECT posnr matnr kwmeng FROM vbap
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_items)
    WHERE vbeln = @lv_order.
  " Map to et_entityset
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you document authorization requirements when converting FS to TS for OData?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS authorization requirements TS mein auth objects mein translate hoti hain. OData ke liye, main do layers document karta hoon: service-level auth (service name aur type HT ke saath S_SERVICE — kisi bhi user ko service call karne ke liye PFCG role mein needed) aur data-level auth (standard SAP auth objects jaise sales orders ke liye V_VBAK_VKO). DPC_EXT mein, main ya toh SELECT mein SAP standard authorization pe rely karta hoon (SAP kernel automatically VBAK se SELECT ke dauraan standard auth check karta hai) ya custom tables ke liye explicit AUTHORITY-CHECK statements add karta hoon. Main document karta hoon ki kaunse objects checked hain, kaunse fields, aur auth failures ke liye error handling kya hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: FS authorization requirements translate to auth objects in TS. For OData, I document two layers: service-level auth (S_SERVICE with service name and type HT — needed in PFCG role for any user to call the service) and data-level auth (standard SAP auth objects like V_VBAK_VKO for sales orders). In DPC_EXT, I either rely on SAP standard authorization in SELECT (SAP kernel automatically checks standard auth during SELECT from VBAK) or add explicit AUTHORITY-CHECK statements for custom tables. I document which objects are checked, which fields, and what the error handling is for auth failures.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Auth is often discovered late in projects. TS auth documentation ensures security review happens before UAT, not after go-live. SAP GRC team uses the TS auth section to configure roles and SoD.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Not documenting auth in TS and leaving it to 'figure out during testing' — causes UAT delays when users start getting 403 errors. Document auth objects in TS, validate with security team during design review.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS auth section for SALESORDER service:

" Layer 1 - Service access (all users of the service):
" Auth Object: S_SERVICE
"   SRV_NAME: ZSALES_SRV_0001
"   SRV_TYPE: HT
" Action: Add to PFCG role for all Fiori users

" Layer 2 - Data access (sales org restriction):
" Auth Object: V_VBAK_VKO
"   VKORG: user's assigned sales org
"   VTWEG: distribution channel
"   ACTVT: 03 (display)
" Checked by: SAP kernel during SELECT from VBAK
" Error: user sees only their org's orders (automatic)

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle OData data type mapping when writing TS from FS?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TS likhte time, main ABAP DDIC field types ko OData EDM types ke saath map karta hoon. String (CHAR, NUMC): ABAP length se matching MaxLength wala Edm.String. Amount (CURR, DEC currency ke saath): Precision aur Scale wala Edm.Decimal. Date (DATS): Edm.DateTime. Boolean (X/space ke saath CHAR 1): Edm.Boolean. Quantity (QUAN): Edm.Decimal. Main amount fields map karte time currency key field document karta hoon — OData proper Fiori formatting ke liye Edm.Decimal amounts ke saath separate Currency property associated require karta hai. SEGW mein, main 'Import ABAP Structure' use karta hoon types auto-map karne ke liye aur phir jahan needed ho adjust karta hoon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When writing TS, I map ABAP DDIC field types to OData EDM types. String (CHAR, NUMC): Edm.String with MaxLength matching the ABAP length. Amount (CURR, DEC with currency): Edm.Decimal with Precision and Scale. Date (DATS): Edm.DateTime. Boolean (CHAR 1 with X/space): Edm.Boolean. Quantity (QUAN): Edm.Decimal. I document the currency key field when mapping amount fields — OData requires a separate Currency property associated with Edm.Decimal amounts for proper Fiori formatting. In SEGW, I use 'Import ABAP Structure' to auto-map types and then adjust where needed.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Amount fields without associated currency key field cause Fiori formatting issues — amounts display without currency symbol or in wrong format. Always map amount fields with their currency key (WAERK for VBAK amounts).
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Mapping DATS (date) to Edm.String — dates display as '20240101' string in Fiori instead of a proper date. Always use Edm.DateTime for ABAP DATS fields.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS data type mapping table:
" ABAP Field   DDIC Type   OData Type          Notes
" VBELN        CHAR(10)    Edm.String(10)      Order number
" NETWR        CURR(13,2)  Edm.Decimal(15,2)   Amount
" WAERK        CUKY(5)     Edm.String(5)       Currency key
" ERDAT        DATS(8)     Edm.DateTime        Order date
" GBSTK        CHAR(1)     Edm.String(1)       Status char
" KWMENG       QUAN(13,3)  Edm.Decimal(13,3)   Quantity

" SEGW: Import from ABAP Structure for auto-mapping
" Entity Type → Properties → Import → ABAP Structure

followupAnswerBank.question: What questions should you ask the functional consultant when you receive a vague FS for OData?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab FS vague ho, main functional consultant se puchta hoon: Kaunse fields screen pe required hain versus nice-to-have (entity properties list drive karta hai)? Kaunse filters mandatory hain versus optional (DPC_EXT mein filter validation drive karta hai)? Per user session expected data volume kya hai ($top limits aur mandatory filter decisions drive karta hai)? Kya hume related documents jaise line items ya deliveries pe navigation chahiye (association design drive karta hai)? Kya create ya update operations required hain (kaunse DPC_EXT methods implement karne hain yeh drive karta hai)? Role ke according kaunsi authorization restrictions apply karti hain (auth object selection drive karta hai)? Yeh answers vague FS ko precise TS mein transform karte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When FS is vague, I ask the functional consultant: Which fields are required on screen versus nice-to-have (drives entity properties list)? Which filters are mandatory versus optional (drives filter validation in DPC_EXT)? What is the expected data volume per user session (drives $top limits and mandatory filter decisions)? Do we need navigation to related documents like line items or deliveries (drives association design)? Are create or update operations required (drives which DPC_EXT methods to implement)? What authorization restrictions apply by role (drives auth object selection)? These answers transform a vague FS into a precise TS.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS review meeting is an investment — 1 hour clarifying before development saves 5-10 hours of rework. Make it a standard practice: 'I will write the TS draft after this meeting and share for your review before coding starts.'
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assuming answers to unclear FS questions and coding based on assumptions — classic cause of rework. Always clarify ambiguities before TS is finalized.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FS review checklist (before writing TS):
" [ ] Field list confirmed (entity properties)
" [ ] Mandatory vs optional filters confirmed
" [ ] Data volume estimate received
" [ ] Navigation requirements (related entities) confirmed
" [ ] CRUD operations scope confirmed (read-only vs CRUD)
" [ ] User roles and auth restrictions confirmed
" [ ] Device requirements (desktop/mobile/tablet)
" [ ] Error message requirements documented
" [ ] UAT data availability confirmed

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you write the entity key definition when converting FS to TS for OData?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS se, main identify karta hoon ki har business object ko kya uniquely identify karta hai. SalesOrder ke liye, VBELN single key hai. SalesOrderItem ke liye, VBELN aur POSNR together composite key form karte hain. TS mein main key field names, data types, aur lengths document karta hoon. SEGW mein, key properties Is Key flag ke saath marked hote hain. Composite key OData URLs named notation use karte hain: /SalesOrderItemSet(SalesOrder='12345',ItemNumber='000010'). Incorrect key definition GET_ENTITY aur navigation failures cause karta hai, isliye main hamesha SE11 table primary key definition ke against keys confirm karta hoon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: From the FS, I identify what uniquely identifies each business object. For SalesOrder, VBELN is the single key. For SalesOrderItem, VBELN and POSNR together form the composite key. In TS I document key field names, data types, and lengths. In SEGW, key properties are marked with the Is Key flag. Composite key OData URLs use named notation: /SalesOrderItemSet(SalesOrder='12345',ItemNumber='000010'). Incorrect key definition causes GET_ENTITY and navigation failures, so I always confirm keys against SE11 table primary key definition.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: SEGW generates key-based URL patterns automatically from your key definitions. Getting keys wrong early means changing them later breaks generated code — costly rework. Nail the key design in TS.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Using wrong key fields — for example using internal ID instead of the business document number displayed to users. Confirm key fields with functional consultant: 'Is this the number users will use to identify the record?'
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS key definitions:
" Entity: SalesOrder
" Key: SalesOrder VBELN Edm.String MaxLength=10 IsKey=true
" URL: /SalesOrderSet('0000012345')

" Entity: SalesOrderItem (composite key)
" Keys: SalesOrder VBELN Edm.String MaxLength=10 IsKey=true
"       ItemNumber  POSNR Edm.String MaxLength=6  IsKey=true
" URL: /SalesOrderItemSet(SalesOrder='12345',ItemNumber='000010')

" SE11 verify: primary key of VBAK = MANDT + VBELN
" (MANDT excluded from OData — client always current)
